wine_festivals.name: Rheingauer Weinwoche Wiesbaden
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, ten consecutive days from the second Friday in August
wine_festivals.description: Ten days of Rheingau wine on Wiesbaden's Schlossplatz from the second Friday in August. Around 100 stands pour Rheingau Riesling, Spaetburgunder and Sekt directly from growers across the region.
wine_festivals.tip: Pace yourself over two evenings; the 100-stand layout under the Stadtschloss is too big for a single visit, and the square cools fast after dark.

wine_festivals.name: Rheingau Musik Festival
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, late June to early September
wine_festivals.neighborhood: across the Rheingau (Kloster Eberbach, Schloss Johannisberg, Schloss Vollrads and more)
wine_festivals.description: A summer concert series from late June to early September across Rheingau wine estates and the Kloster Eberbach abbey, with classical, jazz and crossover programmes paired with estate Riesling and Spaetburgunder.
wine_festivals.tip: Book seats early for concerts inside the Kloster Eberbach basilica; the 12th-century church acoustics are the headline of the season.

wine_festivals.name: Erdbeerfest Erbach
wine_festivals.description: A mid-June strawberry and wine festival in Erbach, part of the city of Eltville, pairing local strawberries with Rheingau Riesling and Spaetburgunder rose across a long weekend by the Rhine.
wine_festivals.tip: Drive over from Hattenheim or Oestrich-Winkel; Erbach is small, and parking on festival evenings fills fast, so go early.

wine_festivals.name: Sektfest Eltville
wine_festivals.description: A first-weekend-in-July sparkling-wine festival on the Eltville riverfront, with stands from Sekt houses including Schloss Vaux, Wegeler and other local Sekt makers pouring traditional-method Riesling and Spaetburgunder Sekt.
wine_festivals.tip: Combine the festival with a guided tour at Schloss Vaux a few streets back; both lean on traditional-method Riesling Sekt.

wine_festivals.name: Rauenthaler Weinfest
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual, second or third weekend in August
wine_festivals.description: A second- or third-weekend-in-August village wine festival in Rauenthal above Eltville, with growers from the Rauenthaler Baiken and neighbouring sites pouring estate Riesling on the village street.
wine_festivals.tip: Rauenthal sits on the hill above Eltville with vineyard views over the Rhine; walk up before sundown rather than driving.

wine_festivals.name: Kappeskerb and Weinlesefest Eltville
wine_festivals.description: The Kappeskerb Weinlesefest in Eltville closes the harvest year on the last weekend in October, with a wine market and harvest fair through the old town along the Rhine.
wine_festivals.tip: Try the just-pressed Federweisser at the harvest stands; the cabbage-and-pork dishes named in Kappeskerb pair well with off-dry Riesling.

wine_festivals.name: Martinsthaler Weinfest
wine_festivals.description: Second-weekend-in-July village wine festival in Martinsthal, a small Eltville district above the Rhine, pouring grower Riesling from the Martinsthaler Wildsau and surrounding slopes.
wine_festivals.tip: Martinsthal is the quietest of the Eltville district festivals; come for the village feel and the by-the-glass Wildsau Riesling.

wine_festivals.name: Hochheimer Markt
wine_festivals.recurrence_pattern: annual since 1484, early November Friday to Tuesday
wine_festivals.description: An early-November autumn market held in Hochheim since 1484, mixing fairground, livestock and craft stalls with Rheingau wine pours over a long Friday-to-Tuesday weekend that draws close to half a million visitors.
wine_festivals.tip: Pair the fair with a tasting at Weingut Kuenstler or Domdechant Werner a short walk away to see why Hochheim is the Rheingau's eastern wine anchor.

wine_festivals.name: Kiedricher Rieslingfest
wine_festivals.description: A September village wine festival in Kiedrich pouring estate Riesling from local growers including Robert Weil, with stands across the church square in the historic Rheingau wine village.
wine_festivals.tip: Walk up the hill afterwards to the Robert Weil estate gates; the village is small enough to combine the festival with a Weil tasting in one afternoon.

wine_festivals.name: Kiedricher Champagnerfest
wine_festivals.description: A first-Sunday-in-June Champagne festival in Kiedrich, organised by the village's friendship association with Hautvillers in Champagne, with sparkling pours and local Rheingau Riesling Sekt alongside grower Champagne.
wine_festivals.tip: The Hautvillers twinning makes this rare in the Rheingau, a day to compare grower Champagne with German Sekt under one tent.

wine_festivals.name: Ruedesheimer Federweisser- und Zwiebelkuchenfest
wine_festivals.description: Three October weekends of Federweisser and Zwiebelkuchen on Ruedesheim's Drosselgasse and Marktplatz, with new-wine stands, onion-tart kitchens and live brass-band music through the old town.
wine_festivals.tip: Federweisser ferments in the cup, so drink it fresh and warm; pair every glass with a slice of Zwiebelkuchen from the church-square stands.
